NKJV.1: Oh, give thanks to the LORD! Call upon His name;  Make known His deeds among the peoples!
NKJV.2: Sing to Him, sing psalms to Him;  Talk of all His wondrous works!
NKJV.3: Glory in His holy name; Let the hearts of those rejoice who seek the LORD!
NKJV.4: Seek the LORD and His strength;  Seek His face evermore!
NKJV.5: Remember His marvelous works which He has done, His wonders, and the judgments of His mouth,
NKJV.6: O seed of Abraham His servant, You children of Jacob, His chosen ones!
NKJV.7: He is the LORD our God; His judgments are in all the earth.
NKJV.8: He remembers His covenant forever, The word which He commanded, for a thousand generations,
NKJV.9: The covenant which He made with Abraham, And His oath to Isaac,
NKJV.10: And confirmed it to Jacob for a statute, To Israel as an everlasting covenant,
NKJV.11: Saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan As the allotment of your inheritance,”
NKJV.12: When they were few in number, Indeed very few, and strangers in it.
NKJV.13: When they went from one nation to another, From one kingdom to another people,
NKJV.14: He permitted no one to do them wrong; Yes, He rebuked kings for their sakes,
NKJV.15: Saying, “Do not touch My anointed ones, And do My prophets no harm.”
NKJV.16: Moreover He called for a famine in the land; He destroyed all the provision of bread.
NKJV.17: He sent a man before them— Joseph—who was sold as a slave.
NKJV.18: They hurt his feet with fetters, He was laid in irons.
NKJV.19: Until the time that his word came to pass,  The word of the LORD tested him.
NKJV.20: The king sent and released him, The ruler of the people let him go free.
NKJV.21: He made him lord of his house, And ruler of all his possessions,
NKJV.22: To bind his princes at his pleasure, And teach his elders wisdom.
NKJV.23: Israel also came into Egypt, And Jacob dwelt in the land of Ham.
NKJV.24: He increased His people greatly, And made them stronger than their enemies.
NKJV.25: He turned their heart to hate His people, To deal craftily with His servants.
NKJV.26: He sent Moses His servant, And Aaron whom He had chosen.
NKJV.27: They performed His signs among them, And wonders in the land of Ham.
NKJV.28: He sent darkness, and made it dark; And they did not rebel against His word.
NKJV.29: He turned their waters into blood, And killed their fish.
NKJV.30: Their land abounded with frogs, Even in the chambers of their kings.
NKJV.31: He spoke, and there came swarms of flies, And lice in all their territory.
NKJV.32: He gave them hail for rain, And flaming fire in their land.
NKJV.33: He struck their vines also, and their fig trees, And splintered the trees of their territory.
NKJV.34: He spoke, and locusts came, Young locusts without number,
NKJV.35: And ate up all the vegetation in their land, And devoured the fruit of their ground.
NKJV.36: He also destroyed all the firstborn in their land,  The first of all their strength.
NKJV.37: He also brought them out with silver and gold, And there was none feeble among His tribes.
NKJV.38: Egypt was glad when they departed, For the fear of them had fallen upon them.
NKJV.39: He spread a cloud for a covering, And fire to give light in the night.
NKJV.40: The people asked, and He brought quail, And satisfied them with the bread of heaven.
NKJV.41: He opened the rock, and water gushed out; It ran in the dry places like a river.
NKJV.42: For He remembered His holy promise, And Abraham His servant.
NKJV.43: He brought out His people with joy, His chosen ones with gladness.
NKJV.44: He gave them the lands of the Gentiles, And they inherited the labor of the nations,
NKJV.45: That they might observe His statutes And keep His laws. Praise the LORD!

KJV.44: And gave<S>5414</S> them the lands<S>776</S> of the heathen<S>1471</S>: and they inherited<S>3423</S> the labour<S>5999</S> of the people<S>3816</S>;
KJV.45: That they might observe<S>8104</S> his statutes<S>2706</S>, and keep<S>5341</S> his laws<S>8451</S>. Praise<S>1984</S> ye the LORD<S>3050</S>. <sup>Praise: Heb. Hallelujah</sup>

ESV.1: Oh give thanks to the LORD; call upon his name; make known his deeds among the peoples!
ESV.2: Sing to him, sing praises to him; tell of all his wondrous works!
ESV.3: Glory in his holy name; let the hearts of those who seek the LORD rejoice!
ESV.4: Seek the LORD and his strength; seek his presence continually!
ESV.5: Remember the wondrous works that he has done, his miracles, and the judgments he uttered,
ESV.6: O offspring of Abraham, his servant, children of Jacob, his chosen ones!
ESV.7: He is the LORD our God; his judgments are in all the earth.
ESV.8: He remembers his covenant forever, the word that he commanded, for a thousand generations,
ESV.9: the covenant that he made with Abraham, his sworn promise to Isaac,
ESV.10: which he confirmed to Jacob as a statute, to Israel as an everlasting covenant,
ESV.11: saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan as your portion for an inheritance.”
ESV.12: When they were few in number, of little account, and sojourners in it,
ESV.13: wandering from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another people,
ESV.14: he allowed no one to oppress them; he rebuked kings on their account,
ESV.15: saying, “Touch not my anointed ones, do my prophets no harm!”
ESV.16: When he summoned a famine on the land and broke all supply  of bread,
ESV.17: he had sent a man ahead of them, Joseph, who was sold as a slave.
ESV.18: His feet were hurt with fetters; his neck was put in a collar of iron;
ESV.19: until what he had said came to pass, the word of the LORD tested him.
ESV.20: The king sent and released him; the ruler of the peoples set him free;
ESV.21: he made him lord of his house and ruler of all his possessions,
ESV.22: to bind  his princes at his pleasure and to teach his elders wisdom.
ESV.23: Then Israel came to Egypt; Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.
ESV.24: And the LORD made his people very fruitful and made them stronger than their foes.
ESV.25: He turned their hearts to hate his people, to deal craftily with his servants.
ESV.26: He sent Moses, his servant, and Aaron, whom he had chosen.
ESV.27: They performed his signs among them and miracles in the land of Ham.
ESV.28: He sent darkness, and made the land dark; they did not rebel  against his words.
ESV.29: He turned their waters into blood and caused their fish to die.
ESV.30: Their land swarmed with frogs, even in the chambers of their kings.
ESV.31: He spoke, and there came swarms of flies, and gnats throughout their country.
ESV.32: He gave them hail for rain, and fiery lightning bolts through their land.
ESV.33: He struck down their vines and fig trees, and shattered the trees of their country.
ESV.34: He spoke, and the locusts came, young locusts without number,
ESV.35: which devoured all the vegetation in their land and ate up the fruit of their ground.
ESV.36: He struck down all the firstborn in their land, the firstfruits of all their strength.
ESV.37: Then he brought out Israel with silver and gold, and there was none among his tribes who stumbled.
ESV.38: Egypt was glad when they departed, for dread of them had fallen upon it.
ESV.39: He spread a cloud for a covering, and fire to give light by night.
ESV.40: They asked, and he brought quail, and gave them bread from heaven in abundance.
ESV.41: He opened the rock, and water gushed out; it flowed through the desert like a river.
ESV.42: For he remembered his holy promise, and Abraham, his servant.
ESV.43: So he brought his people out with joy, his chosen ones with singing.
ESV.44: And he gave them the lands of the nations, and they took possession of the fruit of the peoples' toil,
ESV.45: that they might keep his statutes and observe his laws. Praise the LORD!

NASB.1: Oh give thanks to the LORD, call upon His name; Make known His deeds among the peoples.
NASB.2: Sing to Him, sing praises to Him; Speak of all His wonders.
NASB.3: Glory in His holy name; Let the heart of those who seek the LORD be glad.
NASB.4: Seek the LORD and His strength; Seek His face continually.
NASB.5: Remember His wonders which He has done, His marvels and the judgments uttered by His mouth,
NASB.6: O seed of Abraham, His servant, O sons of Jacob, His chosen ones!
NASB.7: He is the LORD our God; His judgments are in all the earth.
NASB.8: He has remembered His covenant forever, The word which He commanded to a thousand generations,
NASB.9: [The] [covenant] which He made with Abraham, And His oath to Isaac.
NASB.10: Then He confirmed it to Jacob for a statute, To Israel as an everlasting covenant,
NASB.11: Saying, 'To you I will give the land of Canaan As the portion of your inheritance,'
NASB.12: When they were only a few men in number, Very few, and strangers in it.
NASB.13: And they wandered about from nation to nation, From [one] kingdom to another people.
NASB.14: He permitted no man to oppress them, And He reproved kings for their sakes:
NASB.15: 'Do not touch My anointed ones, And do My prophets no harm.'
NASB.16: And He called for a famine upon the land; He broke the whole staff of bread.
NASB.17: He sent a man before them, Joseph, [who] was sold as a slave.
NASB.18: They afflicted his feet with fetters, He himself was laid in irons;
NASB.19: Until the time that his word came to pass, The word of the LORD tested him.
NASB.20: The king sent and released him, The ruler of peoples, and set him free.
NASB.21: He made him lord of his house And ruler over all his possessions,
NASB.22: To imprison his princes at will, That he might teach his elders wisdom.
NASB.23: Israel also came into Egypt; Thus Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.
NASB.24: And He caused His people to be very fruitful, And made them stronger than their adversaries.
NASB.25: He turned their heart to hate His people, To deal craftily with His servants.
NASB.26: He sent Moses His servant, [And] Aaron, whom He had chosen.
NASB.27: They performed His wondrous acts among them, And miracles in the land of Ham.
NASB.28: He sent darkness and made [it] dark; And they did not rebel against His words.
NASB.29: He turned their waters into blood And caused their fish to die.
NASB.30: Their land swarmed with frogs [Even] in the chambers of their kings.
NASB.31: He spoke, and there came a swarm of flies [And] gnats in all their territory.
NASB.32: He gave them hail for rain, [And] flaming fire in their land.
NASB.33: He struck down their vines also and their fig trees, And shattered the trees of their territory.
NASB.34: He spoke, and locusts came, And young locusts, even without number,
NASB.35: And ate up all vegetation in their land, And ate up the fruit of their ground.
NASB.36: He also struck down all the firstborn in their land, The first fruits of all their vigor.
NASB.37: Then He brought them out with silver and gold, And among His tribes there was not one who stumbled.
NASB.38: Egypt was glad when they departed, For the dread of them had fallen upon them.
NASB.39: He spread a cloud for a covering, And fire to illumine by night.
NASB.40: They asked, and He brought quail, And satisfied them with the bread of heaven.
NASB.41: He opened the rock and water flowed out; It ran in the dry places [like] a river.
NASB.42: For He remembered His holy word [With] Abraham His servant;
NASB.43: And He brought forth His people with joy, His chosen ones with a joyful shout.
NASB.44: He gave them also the lands of the nations, That they might take possession of [the fruit of] the peoples' labor,
NASB.45: So that they might keep His statutes And observe His laws, Praise the LORD!

NLT.1:
Give thanks to the LORD and proclaim his greatness.
Let the whole world know what he has done.
NLT.2:
Sing to him; yes, sing his praises.
Tell everyone about his wonderful deeds.
NLT.3:
Exult in his holy name;
rejoice, you who worship the LORD.
NLT.4:
Search for the LORD and for his strength;
continually seek him.
NLT.5:
Remember the wonders he has performed,
his miracles, and the rulings he has given,
NLT.6:
you children of his servant Abraham,
you descendants of Jacob, his chosen ones.
NLT.7:
He is the LORD our God.
His justice is seen throughout the land.
NLT.8:
He always stands by his covenant —
the commitment he made to a thousand generations.
NLT.9:
This is the covenant he made with Abraham
and the oath he swore to Isaac.
NLT.10:
He confirmed it to Jacob as a decree,
and to the people of Israel as a never-ending covenant:
NLT.11:
“I will give you the land of Canaan
as your special possession.”
NLT.12:
He said this when they were few in number,
a tiny group of strangers in Canaan.
NLT.13:
They wandered from nation to nation,
from one kingdom to another.
NLT.14:
Yet he did not let anyone oppress them.
He warned kings on their behalf:
NLT.15:
“Do not touch my chosen people,
and do not hurt my prophets.”
NLT.16:
He called for a famine on the land of Canaan,
cutting off its food supply.
NLT.17:
Then he sent someone to Egypt ahead of them —
Joseph, who was sold as a slave.
NLT.18:
They bruised his feet with fetters
and placed his neck in an iron collar.
NLT.19:
Until the time came to fulfill his dreams,
the LORD tested Joseph’s character.
NLT.20:
Then Pharaoh sent for him and set him free;
the ruler of the nation opened his prison door.
NLT.21:
Joseph was put in charge of all the king’s household;
he became ruler over all the king’s possessions.
NLT.22:
He could instruct the king’s aides as he pleased
and teach the king’s advisers.
NLT.23:
Then Israel arrived in Egypt;
Jacob lived as a foreigner in the land of Ham.
NLT.24:
And the LORD multiplied the people of Israel
until they became too mighty for their enemies.
NLT.25:
Then he turned the Egyptians against the Israelites,
and they plotted against the LORD’s servants.
NLT.26:
But the LORD sent his servant Moses,
along with Aaron, whom he had chosen.
NLT.27:
They performed miraculous signs among the Egyptians,
and wonders in the land of Ham.
NLT.28:
The LORD blanketed Egypt in darkness,
for they had defied his commands to let his people go.
NLT.29:
He turned their water into blood,
poisoning all the fish.
NLT.30:
Then frogs overran the land
and even invaded the king’s bedrooms.
NLT.31:
When the LORD spoke, flies descended on the Egyptians,
and gnats swarmed across Egypt.
NLT.32:
He sent them hail instead of rain,
and lightning flashed over the land.
NLT.33:
He ruined their grapevines and fig trees
and shattered all the trees.
NLT.34:
He spoke, and hordes of locusts came —
young locusts beyond number.
NLT.35:
They ate up everything green in the land,
destroying all the crops in their fields.
NLT.36:
Then he killed the oldest son in each Egyptian home,
the pride and joy of each family.
NLT.37:
The LORD brought his people out of Egypt, loaded with silver and gold;
and not one among the tribes of Israel even stumbled.
NLT.38:
Egypt was glad when they were gone,
for they feared them greatly.
NLT.39:
The LORD spread a cloud above them as a covering
and gave them a great fire to light the darkness.
NLT.40:
They asked for meat, and he sent them quail;
he satisfied their hunger with manna — bread from heaven.
NLT.41:
He split open a rock, and water gushed out
to form a river through the dry wasteland.
NLT.42:
For he remembered his sacred promise
to his servant Abraham.
NLT.43:
So he brought his people out of Egypt with joy,
his chosen ones with rejoicing.
NLT.44:
He gave his people the lands of pagan nations,
and they harvested crops that others had planted.
NLT.45:
All this happened so they would follow his decrees
and obey his instructions.
Praise the LORD!

WEB.1: Give thanks to Yahweh! Call on his name! Make his doings known among the peoples.
WEB.2: Sing to him, sing praises to him! Tell of all his marvelous works.
WEB.3: Glory in his holy name. Let the heart of those who seek Yahweh rejoice.
WEB.4: Seek Yahweh and his strength. Seek his face forever more.
WEB.5: Remember his marvelous works that he has done; his wonders, and the judgments of his mouth,
WEB.6: you offspring  of Abraham, his servant, you children of Jacob, his chosen ones.
WEB.7: He is Yahweh, our God. His judgments are in all the earth.
WEB.8: He has remembered his covenant forever, the word which he commanded to a thousand generations,
WEB.9: the covenant which he made with Abraham, his oath to Isaac,
WEB.10: and confirmed the same to Jacob for a statute; to Israel for an everlasting covenant,
WEB.11: saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan, the lot of your inheritance”;
WEB.12: when they were but a few men in number, yes, very few, and foreigners in it.
WEB.13: They went about from nation to nation, from one kingdom to another people.
WEB.14: He allowed no one to do them wrong. Yes, he reproved kings for their sakes,
WEB.15: “Don’t touch my anointed ones! Do my prophets no harm!”
WEB.16: He called for a famine on the land. He destroyed the food supplies.
WEB.17: He sent a man before them. Joseph was sold for a slave.
WEB.18: They bruised his feet with shackles. His neck was locked in irons,
WEB.19: until the time that his word happened, and Yahweh’s word proved him true.
WEB.20: The king sent and freed him; even the ruler of peoples, and let him go free.
WEB.21: He made him lord of his house, and ruler of all of his possessions;
WEB.22: to discipline his princes at his pleasure, and to teach his elders wisdom.
WEB.23: Israel also came into Egypt. Jacob lived in the land of Ham.
WEB.24: He increased his people greatly, and made them stronger than their adversaries.
WEB.25: He turned their heart to hate his people, to conspire against his servants.
WEB.26: He sent Moses, his servant, and Aaron, whom he had chosen.
WEB.27: They performed miracles among them, and wonders in the land of Ham.
WEB.28: He sent darkness, and made it dark. They didn’t rebel against his words.
WEB.29: He turned their waters into blood, and killed their fish.
WEB.30: Their land swarmed with frogs, even in the rooms of their kings.
WEB.31: He spoke, and swarms of flies came, and lice in all their borders.
WEB.32: He gave them hail for rain, with lightning in their land.
WEB.33: He struck their vines and also their fig trees, and shattered the trees of their country.
WEB.34: He spoke, and the locusts came, and the grasshoppers, without number,
WEB.35: ate up every plant in their land; and ate up the fruit of their ground.
WEB.36: He struck also all the firstborn in their land, the first fruits of all their manhood.
WEB.37: He brought them out with silver and gold. There was not one feeble person among his tribes.
WEB.38: Egypt was glad when they departed, for the fear of them had fallen on them.
WEB.39: He spread a cloud for a covering, fire to give light in the night.
WEB.40: They asked, and he brought quails, and satisfied them with the bread of the sky.
WEB.41: He opened the rock, and waters gushed out. They ran as a river in the dry places.
WEB.42: For he remembered his holy word, and Abraham, his servant.
WEB.43: He brought his people out with joy, his chosen with singing.
WEB.44: He gave them the lands of the nations. They took the labor of the peoples in possession,
WEB.45: that they might keep his statutes, and observe his laws. Praise Yah!

CSB17.1:
Give thanks to the LORD, call on his name;
proclaim his deeds among the peoples. ⓑ
CSB17.2:
Sing to him, sing praise to him;
tell about all his wondrous works! ⓒ
CSB17.3:
Honor his holy name;
let the hearts of those who seek the LORD rejoice. ⓓ
CSB17.4:
Seek the LORD and his strength;
seek his face always. ⓔ
CSB17.5:
Remember the wondrous works he has done,
his wonders, and the judgments he has pronounced, [1] ⓕ
CSB17.6:
you offspring of Abraham his servant,
Jacob’s descendants — his chosen ones. ⓖ
CSB17.7:
He is the LORD our God;
his judgments govern the whole earth. ⓗ
CSB17.8:
He remembers his covenant forever,
the promise he ordained
for a thousand generations ⓘ  —
CSB17.9:
the covenant he made with Abraham,
swore [2] to Isaac, ⓙ
CSB17.10:
and confirmed to Jacob as a decree
and to Israel as a permanent covenant: ⓚ
CSB17.11:
“I will give the land of Canaan to you
as your inherited portion.” ⓛ
CSB17.12:
When they were few in number,
very few indeed,
and resident aliens in Canaan, ⓜ
CSB17.13:
wandering from nation to nation
and from one kingdom to another, ⓝ
CSB17.14:
he allowed no one to oppress them;
he rebuked kings on their behalf: ⓐ
CSB17.15:
“Do not touch my anointed ones, ⓑ
or harm my prophets.” ⓒ
CSB17.16:
He called down famine against the land
and destroyed the entire food supply. ⓓ
CSB17.17:
He had sent a man ahead of them —
Joseph, who was sold as a slave. ⓔ
CSB17.18:
They hurt his feet with shackles;
his neck was put in an iron collar. ⓕ
CSB17.19:
Until the time his prediction came true,
the word of the LORD tested him. ⓖ
CSB17.20:
The king sent for him and released him;
the ruler of peoples set him free. ⓗ
CSB17.21:
He made him master of his household,
ruler over all his possessions ⓘ  —
CSB17.22:
binding [3] his officials at will
and instructing his elders. ⓙ
CSB17.23:
Then Israel went to Egypt;
Jacob lived as an alien in the land of Ham. [4] ⓚ
CSB17.24:
The LORD [5] made his people very fruitful;
he made them more numerous than their foes, ⓛ
CSB17.25:
whose hearts he turned to hate his people
and to deal deceptively with his servants. ⓜ
CSB17.26:
He sent Moses his servant,
and Aaron, whom he had chosen. ⓝ
CSB17.27:
They performed his miraculous signs among them,
and wonders in the land of Ham. ⓐ
CSB17.28:
He sent darkness, and it became dark —
for did they not defy his commands? ⓑ
CSB17.29:
He turned their water into blood
and caused their fish to die. ⓒ
CSB17.30:
Their land was overrun with frogs,
even in their royal chambers. ⓓ
CSB17.31:
He spoke, and insects came ⓔ  —
gnats throughout their country. ⓕ
CSB17.32:
He gave them hail for rain,
and lightning throughout their land. ⓖ
CSB17.33:
He struck their vines and fig trees
and shattered the trees of their territory. ⓗ
CSB17.34:
He spoke, and locusts came —
young locusts without number.
CSB17.35:
They devoured all the vegetation in their land
and consumed the produce of their land. ⓘ
CSB17.36:
He struck all the firstborn in their land,
all their first progeny. ⓙ
CSB17.37:
Then he brought Israel out with silver and gold,
and no one among his tribes stumbled. ⓚ
CSB17.38:
Egypt was glad when they left,
for the dread of Israel [6] had fallen on them. ⓛ
CSB17.39:
He spread a cloud as a covering
and gave a fire to light up the night. ⓜ
CSB17.40:
They asked, and he brought quail
and satisfied them with bread from heaven. ⓝ
CSB17.41:
He opened a rock, and water gushed out;
it flowed like a stream in the desert. ⓐ
CSB17.42:
For he remembered his holy promise
to Abraham his servant. ⓑ
CSB17.43:
He brought his people out with rejoicing,
his chosen ones with shouts of joy. ⓒ
CSB17.44:
He gave them the lands of the nations,
and they inherited
what other peoples had worked for. ⓓ
CSB17.45:
All this happened
so that they might keep his statutes
and obey his instructions. ⓔ
Hallelujah!

AMP.1:
O give thanks to the LORD, call upon His name;
Make known His deeds among the people.
AMP.2:
Sing to Him, sing praises to Him;
Speak of all His wonderful acts and devoutly praise them.
AMP.3:
Glory in His holy name;
Let the hearts of those who seek and require the LORD [as their most essential need] rejoice.
AMP.4:
Seek and deeply long for the LORD and His strength [His power, His might];
Seek and deeply long for His face and His presence continually.
AMP.5:
Remember [with awe and gratitude] the wonderful things which He has done,
His amazing deeds and the judgments uttered by His mouth [on His enemies, as in Egypt],
AMP.6:
O you offspring of Abraham, His servant,
O you sons of Jacob, His chosen ones!
AMP.7:
He is the LORD our God;
His judgments are in all the earth.
AMP.8:
He has remembered His covenant forever,
The word which He commanded and established to a thousand generations,
AMP.9:
The covenant which He made with Abraham,
And His sworn oath to Isaac,
AMP.10:
Which He confirmed to Jacob as a statute,
To Israel as an everlasting covenant,
AMP.11:
Saying, "To you I will give the land of Canaan
As the measured portion of your inheritance."
AMP.12:
When there were only a few men in number,
Very few [in fact], and strangers in it;
AMP.13:
And they wandered from one nation to another,
From one kingdom to another people,
AMP.14:
He allowed no man to oppress them;
He rebuked kings for their sakes, saying,
AMP.15:
"Do not touch My anointed ones,
And do My prophets no harm."
AMP.16:
And He called for a famine upon the land [of Egypt];
He cut off every source of bread.
AMP.17:
He sent a man before them,
Joseph, who was sold as a slave.
AMP.18:
His feet they hurt with shackles;
He was put in chains of iron,
AMP.19:
Until the time that his word [of prophecy regarding his brothers] came true,
The word of the LORD tested and refined him.
AMP.20:
The king sent and released him,
The ruler of the peoples [of Egypt], and set him free.
AMP.21:
He made Joseph lord of his house
And ruler of all his possessions,
AMP.22:
To imprison his princes at his will,
That he might teach his elders wisdom.
AMP.23:
Israel also came into Egypt;
Thus Jacob sojourned in the land of Ham.
AMP.24:
There the LORD greatly increased [the number of] His people,
And made them more powerful than their enemies.
AMP.25:
He turned the heart [of the Egyptians] to hate His people,
To deal craftily with His servants.
AMP.26:
He sent Moses His servant,
And Aaron, whom He had chosen.
AMP.27:
They exhibited His wondrous signs among them,
Great miracles in the land of Ham (Egypt).
AMP.28:
He sent [thick, oppressive] darkness and made the land dark;
And Moses and Aaron did not rebel against His words.
AMP.29:
He turned Egypt's waters into blood
And caused their fish to die.
AMP.30:
Their land swarmed with frogs,
Even in the chambers of their kings.
AMP.31:
He spoke, and there came swarms of flies
And gnats in all their territory.
AMP.32:
He gave them hail for rain,
With flaming fire in their land.
AMP.33:
He struck their vines also and their fig trees,
And shattered the [ice-laden] trees of their territory.
AMP.34:
He spoke, and the [migratory] locusts came,
And the young locusts, even without number,
AMP.35:
And ate up all the vegetation in their land,
And devoured the fruit of their ground.
AMP.36:
He also struck down all the firstborn in their land,
The first fruits and chief substance of all their strength.
AMP.37:
He brought the sons of Israel out [of Egypt] with silver and gold,
And among their tribes there was not one who stumbled.
AMP.38:
Egypt was glad when they departed,
For the dread and fear of them had fallen on the Egyptians.
AMP.39:
The LORD spread a cloud as a covering [by day],
And a fire to illumine the night.
AMP.40:
The Israelites asked, and He brought quail,
And satisfied them with the bread of heaven.
AMP.41:
He opened the rock and water flowed out;
It ran in the dry places like a river.
AMP.42:
For He remembered His holy word
To Abraham His servant;
AMP.43:
He brought out His people with joy,
And His chosen ones with a joyful shout,
AMP.44:
He gave them the lands of the nations [of Canaan],
So that they would possess the fruits of those peoples' labor,
AMP.45:
So that they might observe His precepts
And keep His laws [obediently accepting and honoring and valuing them].
Praise the LORD! (Hallelujah!)

MSG.1:
Hallelujah!
Thank God! Pray to him by name!
Tell everyone you meet what he has done!
MSG.2:
Sing him songs, belt out hymns,
translate his wonders into music!
MSG.3:
Honor his holy name with Hallelujahs,
you who seek God. Live a happy life!
MSG.4:
Keep your eyes open for God, watch for his works;
be alert for signs of his presence.
MSG.5:
Remember the world of wonders he has made,
his miracles, and the verdicts he's rendered—
MSG.6:
O seed of Abraham, his servant,
O child of Jacob, his chosen.
MSG.7:
He's God, our God,
in charge of the whole earth.
MSG.8:
And he remembers, remembers his Covenant—
for a thousand generations he's been as good as his word.
MSG.9:
It's the Covenant he made with Abraham,
the same oath he swore to Isaac,
MSG.10:
The very statute he established with Jacob,
the eternal Covenant with Israel,
MSG.11:
Namely, "I give you the land.
Canaan is your hill-country inheritance."
MSG.12:
When they didn't count for much,
a mere handful, and strangers at that,
MSG.13:
Wandering from country to country,
drifting from pillar to post,
MSG.14:
He permitted no one to abuse them.
He told kings to keep their hands off:
MSG.15:
"Don't you dare lay a hand on my anointed,
don't hurt a hair on the heads of my prophets."
MSG.16:
Then he called down a famine on the country,
he broke every last blade of wheat.
MSG.17:
But he sent a man on ahead:
Joseph, sold as a slave.
MSG.18:
They put cruel chains on his ankles,
an iron collar around his neck,
MSG.19:
Until God's word came to the Pharaoh,
and God confirmed his promise.
MSG.20:
God sent the king to release him.
The Pharaoh set Joseph free;
MSG.21:
He appointed him master of his palace,
put him in charge of all his business
MSG.22:
To personally instruct his princes
and train his advisors in wisdom.
MSG.23:
Then Israel entered Egypt,
Jacob immigrated to the Land of Ham.
MSG.24:
God gave his people lots of babies;
soon their numbers alarmed their foes.
MSG.25:
He turned the Egyptians against his people;
they abused and cheated God's servants.
MSG.26:
Then he sent his servant Moses,
and Aaron, whom he also chose.
MSG.27:
They worked marvels in that spiritual wasteland,
miracles in the Land of Ham.
MSG.28:
He spoke, "Darkness!" and it turned dark—
they couldn't see what they were doing.
MSG.29:
He turned all their water to blood
so that all their fish died;
MSG.30:
He made frogs swarm through the land,
even into the king's bedroom;
MSG.31:
He gave the word and flies swarmed,
gnats filled the air.
MSG.32:
He substituted hail for rain,
he stabbed their land with lightning;
MSG.33:
He wasted their vines and fig trees,
smashed their groves of trees to splinters;
MSG.34:
With a word he brought in locusts,
millions of locusts, armies of locusts;
MSG.35:
They consumed every blade of grass in the country
and picked the ground clean of produce;
MSG.36:
He struck down every firstborn in the land,
the first fruits of their virile powers.
MSG.37:
He led Israel out, their arms filled with loot,
and not one among his tribes even stumbled.
MSG.38:
Egypt was glad to have them go—
they were scared to death of them.
MSG.39:
God spread a cloud to keep them cool through the day
and a fire to light their way through the night;
MSG.40:
They prayed and he brought quail,
filled them with the bread of heaven;
MSG.41:
He opened the rock and water poured out;
it flowed like a river through that desert—
MSG.42:
All because he remembered his Covenant,
his promise to Abraham, his servant.
MSG.43:
Remember this! He led his people out singing for joy;
his chosen people marched, singing their hearts out!
MSG.44:
He made them a gift of the country they entered,
helped them seize the wealth of the nations
MSG.45:
So they could do everything he told them—
could follow his instructions to the letter.
Hallelujah!

NIV.1:
Psalm 105
Give thanks to the Lord, call on his name;
make known among the nations what he has done.
NIV.2:
Sing to him, sing praise to him;
tell of all his wonderful acts.
NIV.3:
Glory in his holy name;
let the hearts of those who seek the Lord rejoice.
NIV.4:
Look to the Lord and his strength;
seek his face always.
NIV.5:
Remember the wonders he has done,
his miracles, and the judgments he pronounced,
NIV.6:
O descendants of Abraham his servant,
O sons of Jacob, his chosen ones.
NIV.7:
He is the Lord our God;
his judgments are in all the earth.
NIV.8:
He remembers his covenant forever,
the word he commanded, for a thousand generations,
NIV.9:
the covenant he made with Abraham,
the oath he swore to Isaac.
NIV.10:
He confirmed it to Jacob as a decree,
to Israel as an everlasting covenant:
NIV.11:
“To you I will give the land of Canaan
as the portion you will inherit.”
NIV.12:
When they were but few in number,
few indeed, and strangers in it,
NIV.13:
they wandered from nation to nation,
from one kingdom to another.
NIV.14:
He allowed no one to oppress them;
for their sake he rebuked kings:
NIV.15:
“Do not touch my anointed ones;
do my prophets no harm.”
NIV.16:
He called down famine on the land
and destroyed all their supplies of food;
NIV.17:
and he sent a man before them—
Joseph, sold as a slave.
NIV.18:
They bruised his feet with shackles,
his neck was put in irons,
NIV.19:
till what he foretold came to pass,
till the word of the Lord proved him true.
NIV.20:
The king sent and released him,
the ruler of peoples set him free.
NIV.21:
He made him master of his household,
ruler over all he possessed,
NIV.22:
to instruct his princes as he pleased
and teach his elders wisdom.
NIV.23:
Then Israel entered Egypt;
Jacob lived as an alien in the land of Ham.
NIV.24:
The Lord made his people very fruitful;
he made them too numerous for their foes,
NIV.25:
whose hearts he turned to hate his people,
to conspire against his servants.
NIV.26:
He sent Moses his servant,
and Aaron, whom he had chosen.
NIV.27:
They performed his miraculous signs among them,
his wonders in the land of Ham.
NIV.28:
He sent darkness and made the land dark—
for had they not rebelled against his words?
NIV.29:
He turned their waters into blood,
causing their fish to die.
NIV.30:
Their land teemed with frogs,
which went up into the bedrooms of their rulers.
NIV.31:
He spoke, and there came swarms of flies,
and gnats throughout their country.
NIV.32:
He turned their rain into hail,
with lightning throughout their land;
NIV.33:
he struck down their vines and fig trees
and shattered the trees of their country.
NIV.34:
He spoke, and the locusts came,
grasshoppers without number;
NIV.35:
they ate up every green thing in their land,
ate up the produce of their soil.
NIV.36:
Then he struck down all the firstborn in their land,
the firstfruits of all their manhood.
NIV.37:
He brought out Israel, laden with silver and gold,
and from among their tribes no one faltered.
NIV.38:
Egypt was glad when they left,
because dread of Israel had fallen on them.
NIV.39:
He spread out a cloud as a covering,
and a fire to give light at night.
NIV.40:
They asked, and he brought them quail
and satisfied them with the bread of heaven.
NIV.41:
He opened the rock, and water gushed out;
like a river it flowed in the desert.
NIV.42:
For he remembered his holy promise
given to his servant Abraham.
NIV.43:
He brought out his people with rejoicing,
his chosen ones with shouts of joy;
NIV.44:
he gave them the lands of the nations,
and they fell heir to what others had toiled for—
NIV.45:
that they might keep his precepts
and observe his laws.
Praise the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
O give thanks to the LORD, call on his name,
make known his deeds among the peoples.
NRSVCE.2:
Sing to him, sing praises to him;
tell of all his wonderful works.
NRSVCE.3:
Glory in his holy name;
let the hearts of those who seek the LORD rejoice.
NRSVCE.4:
Seek the LORD and his strength;
seek his presence continually.
NRSVCE.5:
Remember the wonderful works he has done,
his miracles, and the judgments he has uttered,
NRSVCE.6:
O offspring of his servant Abraham,
children of Jacob, his chosen ones.
NRSVCE.7:
He is the LORD our God;
his judgments are in all the earth.
NRSVCE.8:
He is mindful of his covenant forever,
of the word that he commanded, for a thousand generations,
NRSVCE.9:
the covenant that he made with Abraham,
his sworn promise to Isaac,
NRSVCE.10:
which he confirmed to Jacob as a statute,
to Israel as an everlasting covenant,
NRSVCE.11:
saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan
as your portion for an inheritance.”
NRSVCE.12:
When they were few in number,
of little account, and strangers in it,
NRSVCE.13:
wandering from nation to nation,
from one kingdom to another people,
NRSVCE.14:
he allowed no one to oppress them;
he rebuked kings on their account,
NRSVCE.15:
saying, “Do not touch my anointed ones;
do my prophets no harm.”
NRSVCE.16:
When he summoned famine against the land,
and broke every staff of bread,
NRSVCE.17:
he had sent a man ahead of them,
Joseph, who was sold as a slave.
NRSVCE.18:
His feet were hurt with fetters,
his neck was put in a collar of iron;
NRSVCE.19:
until what he had said came to pass,
the word of the LORD kept testing him.
NRSVCE.20:
The king sent and released him;
the ruler of the peoples set him free.
NRSVCE.21:
He made him lord of his house,
and ruler of all his possessions,
NRSVCE.22:
to instruct his officials at his pleasure,
and to teach his elders wisdom.
NRSVCE.23:
Then Israel came to Egypt;
Jacob lived as an alien in the land of Ham.
NRSVCE.24:
And the LORD made his people very fruitful,
and made them stronger than their foes,
NRSVCE.25:
whose hearts he then turned to hate his people,
to deal craftily with his servants.
NRSVCE.26:
He sent his servant Moses,
and Aaron whom he had chosen.
NRSVCE.27:
They performed his signs among them,
and miracles in the land of Ham.
NRSVCE.28:
He sent darkness, and made the land dark;
they rebelled against his words.
NRSVCE.29:
He turned their waters into blood,
and caused their fish to die.
NRSVCE.30:
Their land swarmed with frogs,
even in the chambers of their kings.
NRSVCE.31:
He spoke, and there came swarms of flies,
and gnats throughout their country.
NRSVCE.32:
He gave them hail for rain,
and lightning that flashed through their land.
NRSVCE.33:
He struck their vines and fig trees,
and shattered the trees of their country.
NRSVCE.34:
He spoke, and the locusts came,
and young locusts without number;
NRSVCE.35:
they devoured all the vegetation in their land,
and ate up the fruit of their ground.
NRSVCE.36:
He struck down all the firstborn in their land,
the first issue of all their strength.
NRSVCE.37:
Then he brought Israel out with silver and gold,
and there was no one among their tribes who stumbled.
NRSVCE.38:
Egypt was glad when they departed,
for dread of them had fallen upon it.
NRSVCE.39:
He spread a cloud for a covering,
and fire to give light by night.
NRSVCE.40:
They asked, and he brought quails,
and gave them food from heaven in abundance.
NRSVCE.41:
He opened the rock, and water gushed out;
it flowed through the desert like a river.
NRSVCE.42:
For he remembered his holy promise,
and Abraham, his servant.
NRSVCE.43:
So he brought his people out with joy,
his chosen ones with singing.
NRSVCE.44:
He gave them the lands of the nations,
and they took possession of the wealth of the peoples,
NRSVCE.45:
that they might keep his statutes
and observe his laws.
Praise the LORD!

NET.1: Give thanks to the LORD! Call on his name! Make known his accomplishments among the nations!
NET.2: Sing to him! Make music to him! Tell about all his miraculous deeds!
NET.3: Boast about his holy name! Let the hearts of those who seek the LORD rejoice!
NET.4: Seek the LORD and the strength he gives! Seek his presence continually!
NET.5: Recall the miraculous deeds he performed, his mighty acts and the judgments he decreed,
NET.6: O children of Abraham, God’s servant, you descendants of Jacob, God’s chosen ones!
NET.7: He is the LORD our God; he carries out judgment throughout the earth.
NET.8: He always remembers his covenantal decree, the promise he made to a thousand generations —
NET.9: the promise he made to Abraham, the promise he made by oath to Isaac!
NET.10: He gave it to Jacob as a decree, to Israel as a lasting promise,
NET.11: saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan as the portion of your inheritance.”
NET.12: When they were few in number, just a very few, and resident aliens within it,
NET.13: they wandered from nation to nation, and from one kingdom to another.
NET.14: He let no one oppress them; he disciplined kings for their sake,
NET.15: saying, “Don’t touch my chosen ones! Don’t harm my prophets!”
NET.16: He called down a famine upon the earth; he cut off all the food supply.
NET.17: He sent a man ahead of them — Joseph was sold as a servant.
NET.18: The shackles hurt his feet; his neck was placed in an iron collar,
NET.19: until the time when his prediction came true. The LORD’s word proved him right.
NET.20: The king authorized his release; the ruler of nations set him free.
NET.21: He put him in charge of his palace, and made him manager of all his property,
NET.22: giving him authority to imprison his officials and to teach his advisers.
NET.23: Israel moved to Egypt; Jacob lived for a time in the land of Ham.
NET.24: The LORD made his people very fruitful, and made them more numerous than their enemies.
NET.25: He caused them to hate his people, and to mistreat his servants.
NET.26: He sent his servant Moses, and Aaron, whom he had chosen.
NET.27: They executed his miraculous signs among them, and his amazing deeds in the land of Ham.
NET.28: He made it dark; they did not disobey his orders.
NET.29: He turned their water into blood, and killed their fish.
NET.30: Their land was overrun by frogs, which even got into the rooms of their kings.
NET.31: He ordered flies to come; gnats invaded their whole territory.
NET.32: He sent hail along with the rain; there was lightning in their land.
NET.33: He destroyed their vines and fig trees, and broke the trees throughout their territory.
NET.34: He ordered locusts to come, innumerable grasshoppers.
NET.35: They ate all the vegetation in their land, and devoured the crops of their fields.
NET.36: He struck down all the firstborn in their land, the firstfruits of their reproductive power.
NET.37: He brought his people out enriched with silver and gold; none of his tribes stumbled.
NET.38: Egypt was happy when they left, for they were afraid of them.
NET.39: He spread out a cloud for a cover, and provided a fire to light up the night.
NET.40: They asked for food, and he sent quails; he satisfied them with food from the sky.
NET.41: He opened up a rock and water flowed out; a river ran through dry regions.
NET.42: Yes, he remembered the sacred promise he made to Abraham his servant.
NET.43: When he led his people out, they rejoiced; his chosen ones shouted with joy.
NET.44: He handed the territory of nations over to them, and they took possession of what other peoples had produced,
NET.45: so that they might keep his commands and obey his laws. Praise the LORD!
